title: EndpointType uses Other(String) but BodyType uses Unknown(String) for same concept
description: EndpointType names its catch-all variant Other(String) (line 157) while BodyType names the structurally identical variant Unknown(String) (line 548). Both represent 'unrecognized wire value that must round-trip as-is'. The inconsistency forces readers to remember two names for the same concept. Suggested rewrite: rename EndpointType::Other(String) to EndpointType::Unknown(String) so both enums use the same naming convention. The variant name Other is also less self-documenting than Unknown in the context of spec-defined enums.
close_reason: Valid polish; deferred. Tracked in follow-up bead jmap-chat-tui-yw6v or standalone.
